data.title: Southwest Airlines’ First Major Job Cut in 53 Years Is Set to Reduce 15% Staff
data.description:
Southwest Airlines (LUV) is about to implement its first-ever major job cut in 53 years, reducing 15% of its corporate staff. The layoffs are aimed at cutting costs and streamlining operations. The job cuts will affect roughly 1,750 jobs at the American air carrier, with 11 senior leadership roles being slashed. The front-line workers, including pilots and flight attendants, will not be affected.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Remarkably, the job cuts will help Southwest save $210 million in expenses this year and another $300 million next year. The employees will continue to receive salaries, bonuses, and benefits until April 2025. Hammered by investor pressure, Southwest is forced to take up these drastic restructuring initiatives.

Southwest Airlines’ First Massive Layoff

Southwest has never undertaken a massive layoff in its history. The airline has avoided layoffs even during challenging times, such as the COVID-19 pandemic. In fact, Southwest has been hiring more workforce in recent years to keep up with the growing travel demand. The low-cost carrier has been under pressure from activist investor Elliot Investment Management to improve its performance and boost its stock price.

While announcing the layoffs, CEO Bob Jordan said that LUV’s corporate overhead has been growing faster than the rest of the carrier’s operations. With this effort, the airline is poised to become a leaner organization with efficient workforce management in place. Post the pandemic, airlines worldwide have witnessed a significant surge in travel demand. However, persistent inflation has caused labor and fuel costs to remain elevated, impacting the air carrier’s margins and bottom lines.

Southwest has also announced other transformation initiatives recently such as pausing hiring, promotions, and summer internships. Additionally, LUV started its first red-eye flights to lessen the ground time between flights and bolster sales. It also began offering paid seating options and more legroom. Lower supplies from aircraft manufacturer Boeing (BA) have impacted Southwest’s flight plans, leading to overstaffing issues. Another initiative undertaken by the carrier is the aircraft sale-and-leaseback arrangement with other companies to make money.

Is LUV a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Wall Street prefers to remain on the sidelines on Southwest Airlines stock currently owing to the challenges discussed above. On TipRanks, LUV stock has a Hold consensus rating based on four Buys, seven Holds, and four Sell ratings. Also, the average Southwest Airlines price target of $32.97 implies 8.9% upside potential from current levels. Year to date, LUV stock has lost 9.9%.

See more LUV analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: BYD Faces Consumer Backlash after Free Smart Driving Feature Rollout
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle giant BYD (HK:1211) (BYDDF) faces a surge in consumer complaints after introducing free smart driving features across most of its vehicle lineup on February 10. Between February 11-17, over 4,700 complaints were filed against BYD cars on a Chinese auto consumer complaints platform. This reflects a major increase from about 150 complaints the week before and roughly 500 for January.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Many of these complaints come from customers who feel they overpaid for their vehicles since the new free features were not available at the time of their purchase. The models most affected include those from BYD’s Ocean and Dynasty series.

One notable complaint was from an owner of a Seal 06 DM-i plug-in hybrid sedan, who expressed frustration upon discovering that a newer model with upgrades was launched just two weeks after purchasing his car. The customer sought free upgrades and other compensation from BYD.  

BYD Navigates Intense Competition in China

This rise in complaints reflects the challenges faced by automakers in navigating a highly competitive Chinese EV market. These complaints are a result of the extended price war and intense competition, as automakers continue to cut prices, offer free features or incentives, and launch new models rapidly.

Despite these challenges, BYD holds a dominant share of the Chinese EV market. The company’s strategic decision to introduce its autonomous driving system in at least 21 of its EV models at no additional cost is expected to drive sales growth. Further, BYD remains well poised to benefit from its efforts to expand its presence in international markets.

Is BYDDF a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BYDDF stock based on five Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months. At $45.64, the average BYD Co. stock price target implies a 2.21% downside potential. Year-to-date, shares of the company have gained 37.26%.

See more BYDDF analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Samsung Plans Massive Share Cancellation Worth $2.1B
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) (GB:SMSN) announced a massive cancelation for ₩3 trillion ($2.1 billion) worth of its shares from circulation, according to a company statement released today. The move involves canceling shares that Samsung had purchased from the open market through its buyback program. Additionally, the company will repurchase another ₩3 trillion worth of shares.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Samsung Reshapes Stock Strategy with Buyback and Cancellation

Samsung’s decision to buy back and cancel shares worth ₩3 trillion came after the board resolution passed on November 15 and is part of the company’s broader strategy to boost shareholder value. According to the company’s statement, ₩500 billion will go towards employee bonuses, while ₩2.5 trillion will focus on stabilizing stock prices and enhancing shareholder returns. The company further stated that the shares will be retired based on a board decision using available profits, which will reduce the total number of shares without affecting the company’s capital.

In addition to retiring shares, Samsung plans to acquire more treasury shares for a total value of around ₩3 trillion. The acquisition will occur on the stock market from February 19 to May 16, with ₩2.696 trillion allocated for common shares and ₩303.6 billion for preferred shares.

Samsung Navigates Market Challenges

This announcement comes as Samsung continues to navigate a challenging global tech market. In November last year, Samsung announced plans to repurchase ₩10 trillion worth of shares over the next year, following weaker stock performance compared to competitors. This marked the first time the company had initiated a share buyback since 2017.

In terms of operational performance, Samsung expects a challenging Q1 2025 due to ongoing semiconductor weakness and slow mobile demand. Meanwhile, its Q4 2024 earnings report reflects mixed results. While sales and profits declined due to tough conditions in IT products, the Memory business achieved a record-high revenue for the quarter.

Over the last six months, SMSN stock has declined by almost 34%.

Disclosure
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Airbus Delays A350 Freighter as Supply Woes Hit Production Targets
data.description:
European aerospace manufacturer Airbus (EADSF) is pushing back the launch of its highly anticipated A350 freighter due to ongoing supply chain issues, according to industry reports. The delay, which could stretch up to a year, highlights the challenges the company faces as it struggles to secure key components.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The announcement could be officially made on February 20 when Airbus releases its Q4 and annual results. For the fourth quarter, Wall Street analysts are forecasting earnings of $2.63 per share, which would mark a 20% increase compared to last year. Additionally, revenues are expected to grow by 6% year-over-year, reaching $25.57 billion.

Supply Chain Issues Disrupt Production

The setback is mainly due to ongoing supply chain issues, particularly at Spirit AeroSystems (SPR), a key supplier of fuselage components. These supply issues have slowed Airbus’s production capacity, forcing the company to provide financial assistance to suppliers to help stabilize operations.

Beyond for the freighter, Airbus is also struggling to ramp up production of its A350 passenger jets. The company had aimed to increase output but is currently producing around six planes per month, a rate that may continue into 2026.

The inability to boost production could impact delivery schedules and long-term revenue projections.

Airbus Look to India for Supply Chain Solutions

To tackle some of these supply challenges, Airbus and other companies like Rolls-Royce (RYCEF) are turning to India for advanced parts and sourcing solutions.

Airbus currently buys about €1 billion worth of parts from India each year and plans to double this amount in the coming years, according to the same Reuters report. For example, in 2024, Airbus gave its second aircraft door contract to an Indian supplier. Also, Air India’s recent order for 100 Airbus aircraft shows India’s growing role in the global aerospace supply chain.

Is Airbus Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on EADSF stock based on 11 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. The average EADSF price target of $192.44 per share implies 8.34% upside potential.

See more EADSF analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: ‘Hold Your Horses,’ Says Top Investor About Rigetti Stock
data.description:
Rigetti Computing (NASDAQ:RGTI) stock has been on a tear for the age, fueled by a wave of electrifying enthusiasm for the quantum revolution. Over the past six months, shares have skyrocketed nearly 1,300%, even with a post-CES stumble triggered by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang’s skepticism about quantum computing’s near-term viability.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Though RGTI’s prospects are directly tied to the overall fortunes of the quantum industry, there are plenty of reasons related to the company that have pushed the share prices higher. These include technical advancements such as the Ankaa-3 system, which has successfully achieved a 99.5% two-qubit gate fidelity – a critical threshold for quantum computing.

However, while top investor Yiannis Zourmpanos sees promise in Rigetti, he remains cautious, emphasizing that it is still a ‘show me’ story.

“Future gains hinge on successful execution and funding stability in the evolving quantum landscape,” explains Zourmpanos, who sits in the top 1% of TipRanks’ stock pros.

That being said, the investor acknowledges RGTI’s ambitions, spotlighting the company’s revenue projections north of 40% in the current year. These gains are expected to be driven by the launch of 36-qubit and 108-qubit systems, which the company anticipates leading to a 2x decrease in errors.

Zourmpanos is also encouraged by collaborations with major technology players, which could help grease the wheels for the adoption of quantum computing.

“These partnerships boost Rigetti’s tech capabilities and boost its reach within the quantum computing market,” details Zourmpanos, citing the company’s agreements with Amazon, Microsoft, and Nvidia, among others.

However, despite these positives, the investor notes the significant hurdles RGTI faces going forward, particularly challenges of scale. The company has yet to demonstrate its ability to move beyond 84 qubits, and the transition to larger systems presents challenges related to packaging, testing, and maintaining coherence.

Another concern for the investor is the expiration of the National Quantum Initiative Act, which could result in a loss of funding for the company. While RGTI is actively pursuing contracts with the U.S. Department of Defense, Zourmpanos notes that major competitors like Google and IBM are also vying for these opportunities.

“While advancements in qubit fidelity (99.5% gate fidelity on Ankaa-3) and strategic partnerships strengthen its position, scalability risks, reliance on government contracts, and competition from IBM and Google remain key hurdles,” concludes Zourmpanos, who rates RGTI shares a Hold (i.e., Neutral). (To watch Zourmpanos’ track record, click here)

Wall Street, however, remains smitten with RGTI. With 4 Buy ratings and no Sells, RGTI enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $15.67 implies ~33% upside potential for the year ahead. (See RGTI stock forecast)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanks’ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanks’ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured investor. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Why Blue Bird (BLBD) Remains a Solid Choice Despite Policy Uncertainty
data.description:
Blue Bird Corporation (BLBD) recently announced fiscal Q1 results, beating top and bottom-line expectations. While the company experienced a slight decline in net sales compared to the previous year, its earnings strength remains robust, bolstered by its order backlog of nearly 4,400 units. As a leader in the alternative-powered vehicle segment, the company saw significant growth in EV orders. Uncertainties such as potential tariffs and a halt on federal funding cast its EV sales target for 2025 in doubt. Yet, Blue Bird has maintained its full-year guidance for adjusted EBITDA at $200 million with a 14% margin. The stock trades at a relative discount, making it an intriguing option for value-oriented investors willing to ride the potential policy-induced volatility.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Expanding its Footprint, But Policy Uncertainty is Cause for Caution

Blue Bird is a manufacturer of school buses and parts. Its products include Type C, Type D, and specialty buses. Additionally, it offers buses powered by various energy sources such as propane, gasoline, compressed natural gas, and electricity, aside from traditional diesel engines. Furthermore, the company sells spare parts for bus replacements and offers extended product warranties. The distribution of products is carried out through a network of dealers and direct sales to fleet operators, the United States government, state governments, and independent service centers.

The company has recently announced the appointment of John Wyskiel as its new President and CEO. Wyskiel, a veteran in the automotive industry with over 35 years of experience, will also join the Board of Directors. He is familiar with Blue Bird, having served as their General Manager in Canada from 2002 – 2004. His experience in manufacturing, operations, engineering, and sales is expected to help drive the company’s initiatives and deliver robust growth.

In addition, Blue Bird’s subsidiary, Micro Bird, is set to expand its manufacturing operations in New York State, with plans to begin production in mid-2025. This investment aims to double Micro Bird’s production capacity to meet the increasing demand for their range of buses and multi-purpose vehicles. Funding for this project is supported by the Empire State Development with nearly $10 million in tax credits and a $2.5 million capital grant.

The cooling sentiments surrounding EVs and renewables, intensified by changes in U.S. administration policies, potentially threaten the company’s financial outcomes. Despite a federal judge issuing a temporary restraining order against freezing funding for federal programs, uncertainty remains around the future of clean energy funding, including the EV bus orders under the federally funded Clean School Bus Program. In particular, clarity is lacking on which programs financed by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law will be approved. Key EPA programs remain on hold, sparking confusion among stakeholders and lawmakers pushing for funds to flow to specific initiatives.

Top and Bottom-Line Beats

Blue Bird Corporation recently released its first quarter results of fiscal 2025. The company reported net sales of $313.9 million, exceeding expectations by $5.69 million, though it marked a slight decrease of 1.2% from the same quarter last year. The decline in sales was primarily due to a slight reduction in bus sales, driven by customer and product mix changes. However, on the bright side, parts sales grew by 6.2% due to price increases and higher fulfillment volumes.

Gross profit was $60.3 million, down by $3.2 million compared to the first quarter of last year, primarily due to the decrease in net sales. This was partially offset by a reduction in the cost of goods sold. Net income rose by $2.6 million to reach $28.7 million, primarily due to $2.6 million in emission credits that the company sold in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

Furthermore, adjusted net income was $30.6 million, almost consistent with the previous year. However, adjusted EBITDA fell $1.9 million, primarily due to the lower gross profit, offset to some extent by improvements in other income. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.92 beat analyst expectations by $0.11.

Management has issued forward guidance for 2025, projecting net revenue between $1.4 and $1.5 billion, adjusted EBITDA from $185 to $215 million, and free cash flow between $40 to $60 million. The firm also maintained its long-term projections of an adjusted EBITDA margin above 15%.

Lowered Price Targets

The stock peaked last June and has been on a decline since then. Yet, it is still up over 19% in the past year. It trades in the lower half of its 52-week price range of $29.78 – $59.40 and shows ongoing negative price momentum as it trades below major moving averages. The price decline has driven the stock into relative value territory, with its P/S ratio of 0.87x sitting below the Industrials sector average of 1.57x.

Analysts following the company have been bullish on the stock, though many have lowered their price targets after the recent earnings announcement. For instance, Needham analyst Chris Pierce lowered the price target on shares to $49 (from $66) while maintaining a Buy rating. He noted the trajectory of subsidized EV units has become the overarching story, driving multiple compression.

Blue Bird is rated a Strong Buy overall, based on the recent recommendations of seven analysts. The average price target for BLBD stock is $53.00, which represents a potential upside of 45.80% from current levels.

See more BLBD analyst ratings.

Bottom Line on Blue Bird

Blue Bird’s recent expectation-beating results and growing order backlog point to ongoing upside potential. Despite the stock experiencing a downward trend, analysts remain optimistic and see potential for ongoing growth. While policy uncertainties may introduce some volatility, Blue Bird’s resilience and growth potential make it a potentially appealing choice for value-seeking investors.

Disclosure
data.author: Casey Dylan, CIMA

data.title: MSFT, AMZN, or META: Which “Strong Buy” Mega-Cap Stock Has the Highest Upside Potential?
data.description:
Inflation woes and uncertainties around tariffs are impacting investor sentiment. Investors having a long-term horizon can consider mega-cap stocks, which have a market capitalization of more than $200 billion. These stocks are associated with large, well-established companies with strong track records and generally rank among the leading players in their respective industries. Using TipRanks’ Stock Comparison Tool, we placed Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta Platforms (META) against each other to find the “Strong Buy” mega-cap stock that can deliver the best upside, according to Wall Street analysts.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)

Microsoft is considered to be one of the major AI *artificial intelligence) beneficiaries, with its significant spending on AI and investments in ChatGPT maker OpenAI. Consequently, the market has very high expectations from the company’s cloud computing Azure business. The company’s Q2 FY25 results exceeded Wall Street’s estimates but the 31% growth in Azure and other cloud services failed to impress investors as it marked a slowdown from the 33% growth in the prior quarter.

Azure is facing execution challenges and capacity constraints. Nonetheless, the company expects these pressures to abate and Azure to grow rapidly in the times ahead. During the Q2 FY25 earnings call, the company highlighted that it continues to expand its data center capacity in line with short-term and long-term demand signals. Overall, Microsoft believes that its data centers, networks, racks, and other infrastructure will together drive new efficiencies to support both the existing cloud workloads and the next-generation AI workloads.

The company is also focused on enhancing efficiencies across its businesses, especially given that it is investing significantly to scale its AI infrastructure and build AI applications.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Recently, CFRA analyst Angelo Zino reiterated a Buy rating on Microsoft stock with a price target of $490. The five-star analyst noted that while the company posted better-than-expected results for the December quarter, the outlook for the March quarter slightly lagged CFRA’s estimates due to 200 basis points of forex headwinds and slower-than-projected acceleration in Azure.

On the positive side, Zino highlighted that AI contribution in the December quarter was 13 percentage points of Azure revenue versus CFRA’s estimate of 12 percentage points. Moreover, Zino commented that Copilot’s revenue seems to be surprisingly growing faster than he expected. He pointed out that despite capacity constraints, Microsoft expects to regain balance by the end of the June quarter.

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Microsoft stock based on 29 Buys and three Holds. The average MSFT stock price target of $510.38 implies about 25% upside potential. Shares have been flat over the past year.

See more MSFT analyst ratings

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN)

E-commerce and cloud computing giant Amazon reported market-beating fourth-quarter results. Revenue from the company’s cloud business Amazon Web Services (AWS) increased 19% but slightly fell short of expectations. Investors are concerned that AWS is not growing as rapidly as its peers amid the ongoing AI boom. For instance, Alphabet’s (GOOGL) cloud revenue grew 30% while Microsoft’s revenue from Azure and other cloud services rose 31% in the December quarter.

Further, Amazon’s Q1 2025 outlook missed estimates due to expectations of significant currency headwinds. Notably, the Q1 revenue growth guidance in the range of 5% to 9% marks the slowest growth on record for Amazon.

Nonetheless, the company is confident about the path ahead and has boosted its capex expectations to $100 billion for 2025 from $83 billion spent last year to drive AWS growth.

What Is the Target Price for Amazon Stock?

In reaction to Q4 results, Truist Securities analyst Youssef Squali lowered his price target for Amazon stock to $265 from $270 but maintained a Buy rating. The analyst noted that forex headwinds and higher capex overshadowed Amazon’s strength across retail, AWS, and advertising in Q4. He highlighted that Q1 2025 guidance missed expectations on forex pressures. The analyst said that he was impressed by the North America retail division, which grew faster than overall e-commerce and delivered record segment margin, fueled by improvements in its cost-to-serve.

However, AMZN’s much higher 2025 capex outlook impacted growth and margin expectations for the year. While Squali lowered his estimates and price target, he continues to be bullish on Amazon stock as he views it as one of the best players that is well-positioned to capitalize on the secular growth in e-commerce, Cloud, advertising, and logistics-as-a-service.

Amazon.com stock has jumped about 34% over the past year. The average AMZN stock price target of $268.91 implies about 18% upside potential from current levels. With 47 Buys and one Hold recommendation, Amazon.com stock scores a Strong Buy consensus rating.

See more AMZN analyst ratings

Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META)

Social media giant Meta Platforms delivered upbeat fourth-quarter results, with revenue rising 21% year-over-year. Despite the company’s aggressive planned capital expenditure in the range of $60 billion to $65 billion for 2025, several analysts remain bullish on the stock as they believe in its strong execution and ability to boost its revenue by integrating AI across its family of apps, including Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger.

Meta’s increased capex in 2025 will mainly be directed towards generative AI and the company’s core business. The company is also focused on enhancing its monetization efficiency by improving marketing and capturing untapped opportunities in Threads and other apps.

Is META Stock a Good Buy?

Following the print, Piper Sandler analyst Thomas Champion increased the price target for Meta Platforms stock to $775 from $670 and reiterated a Buy rating. The five-star analyst noted that the company exceeded analysts’ Q4 revenue and EBITDA estimates by 3% and 7%, respectively. Champion added that management sounded energized about core platform improvements and new AI offerings. The analyst noted that while Q1 2025 revenue guidance was a bit weaker than expected, it was mainly due to forex headwinds.

Overall, Meta Platforms stock scores Wall Street’s Strong Buy consensus rating based on 44 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average META stock price target of $764.61 implies about 4% upside potential from current levels. META stock has rallied 56% over the past year.

See more META analyst ratings

Conclusion

Wall Street is bullish on the prospects of Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta Platforms. Analysts see higher upside potential in MSFT stock than in the stocks of the other two mega-caps from current levels. Despite ongoing challenges, analysts are confident about Microsoft’s ability to boost its growth by capturing AI opportunities.

Disclosure
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Nvidia or Alphabet: Billionaire Ken Griffin Bets Big on One Top AI Stock
data.description:
AI mania has taken the world by storm, with the game-changing tech seemingly poised to permeate almost every aspect of our day-to-day lives over the next few years. But is it really as inevitable as it seems?

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

While one fear tied to the rise of AI revolves around concerns it will make many jobs obsolete, Billionaire Ken Griffin is doubtful AI will soon replace human jobs, citing drawbacks in machine learning models when applied to certain situations.

“Machine learning models do not do well in a world where regimes shift,” noted the Citadel founder and CEO. Illustrating his point, he added, “Self-driving cars don’t work very well in the North due to snow. When the terrain changes, they have no idea what to do. Machine learning models do much better when there’s consistency.”

Griffin is no luddite, however, his hedge fund and electronic market-making firm have been leaders in automation, and his skeptical stance does not mean he has been entirely shying away from the AI opportunity.

Lately, the billionaire has been fine-tuning his AI stock portfolio, shuffling his stakes in industry giants Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL). He’s made a big bet on one while trimming his holdings in the other.

So, let’s take a closer look at these two tech colossuses and by delving into the TipRanks database, we can also find out the broader Street view on these names.

Nvidia

Talk of AI and its impact on the stock market and the conversation will naturally turn to Nvidia. While the chip giant was far from a minnow when AI first started making its impact following the release of ChatGPT, Nvidia’s positioning in the space has turned it into one of the world’s most valuable companies.

The reason for that is pretty straightforward; the Jensen Huang-led firm simply makes the best AI chips out there and no other company has so far managed to seriously impact its dominant position. The proof is in the chip pudding: Nvidia commands more than 80% of the AI chip market.

But are cracks starting to appear in the tremendous growth story? One recent headwind has revolved around the delay of its latest GPU architecture, Blackwell, but the company might be facing a threat that seemingly came out of nowhere last month. Chinese startup DeepSeek released its latest AI model with claims it can outperform its US peers. Not only that, it also said it was developed at a fraction of the price, meaning that maybe companies didn’t need to spend enormous sums of money developing their AI models.

What followed was a slice of history with Nvidia losing the biggest chunk of market cap over a single session, shedding $590 billion of its value in one day.

Maybe Griffin sensed a day of reckoning for the high-flying stock was about to play out. During 4Q24, he sold 57% (3,993,858 shares) of his NVDA holdings. It should be noted that he still holds 3,128,677 shares, currently worth more than $434 million.

According to D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria, it might be time to approach the Nvidia story with caution. Looking at the situation following DeepSeek’s noisy entry, he thinks Nvidia might have to face a new reality moving forward.

“We believe we are approaching peak demand, possibly as early as this year as NVDA’s large customers evaluate [the] recent developments,” the 5-star analyst said. “The notion that radically lower prices will led to much higher demand is almost a truism, but in previous instances took time to play out. We believe that lower inference prices will allow enterprises to roll out some new tools faster than they were otherwise planning to, but do not believe there are enough compelling applications for that to increase short-term demand by the 20-30x factor required to make up for the lower need for compute. We see the availability of ready-for-primetime scalable applications as the real gating factor and that may take 2-3 years to materialize. More importantly, as it relates to NVDA, if these new applications get deployed on device, the increased demand may not increase the TAM.”

To this end, Luria rates NVDA shares as Neutral, while his $135 price target suggests the stock will remain rangebound for the time being. (To watch Luria’s track record, click here)

That said, most on the Street are still getting behind the AI chip leader; based on 37 Buys vs. 3 Holds, the stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. Going by the $179.03 average price target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a ~29% premium. (See Nvidia stock forecast)

Alphabet

While Nvidia is a company that so far has benefited immensely from AI’s rise, the tech has been seen as something of an issue for Alphabet – parent company of Google, YouTube, Android, Waymo, DeepMind, and other subsidiaries. 

Despite actually being an AI pioneer, having been using the tech long before the current AI boom, the rise of GenAI is perceived as a real threat to Google’s core Search business. It’s a narrative that makes sense in a way. AI-powered chatbots like ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity are changing how people seek information. Instead of googling something and clicking on links, users can now get information without leaving the platform. This reduces Google’s main bread-and butter – ad revenue, which heavily relies on search traffic. There’s also direct completion from other AI-powered search engines, with Microsoft, for example, integrating AI into Bing.

Alphabet, for its part, has no intention of getting left behind, and has been aggressively expanding its AI capabilities to counter the threat. It has integrated Gemini AI (formerly Bard) into Search and other products and is competing with Microsoft and AWS by offering AI-powered tools for businesses and cloud computing.

That said, the shares fell following the recent Q4 readout with Cloud growth being a particularly sore point. Google Cloud’s revenue fell short of expectations, coming at $11.96 billion vs. the Street’s forecast of $12.19 billion. Additionally, the company forecasted $75 billion in capital expenditures for 2025, far above the consensus estimate of $58.8 billion.

Griffin, however, must think all these worries are overblown. During Q4 he significantly increased his GOOGL holdings by 135% with the purchase of 786,737 shares. His total stake now stands at 1,372,475 shares, worth more than $254 million at the current share price.

Mirroring Griffin’s move, RBC’s Brad Erickson, an analyst ranked in the top 2% of Street stock experts, strikes a confident tone regarding the search giant’s prospects. Following the Q4 results, he wrote, “The company’s AI rollout is accelerating with Search demonstrating improvements as opposed to disruption (consistent with our checks coming in), capacity constraints which likely explain the Cloud miss should ease, Google I/O sets up for a bevy of new AI product announcements and management just lowered the bar on margins given Capex & headcount commentary. No doubt the impact of AI for GOOGL will continue to be hotly debated but intellectual consistency across the space would suggest GOOGL’s playing offense on AI & we’d agree.”

“We’d expect a number of substantive announcements at I/O in early May, however, given the clear breakneck pace of model rollouts in fueling the various products (7 products or platforms now with over 2B users which leverage Gemini), we see the narrative around GOOGL’s overall AI positioning as improving over the medium term versus getting worse,” the 5-star analyst further said.

Conveying his confidence, Erickson rates GOOGL shares as Outperform (i.e., Buy) while his $235 price target makes room for 12-month returns of 27%. (To watch Erickson’s track record, click here)

26 other analysts join Erickson in the bull camp and with the addition of 10 Holds, the consensus view is that this stock is a Moderate Buy. The average target stands at $215.85, suggesting the shares will climb 16.5% higher in the months ahead. (See GOOGL stock forecast)

data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Delta Air Lines (DAL) Plane Crashes at Toronto International Airport
data.description:
A regional jet owned and operated by Delta Air Lines (DAL) that was carrying 80 passengers onboard has crashed at Toronto International Airport in Canada.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The aircraft, a CRJ-900 regional jet, was en route to Toronto from Delta’s hub of Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport when it crashed landed on approach, according to media reports. Apparently all 80 people on board have been evacuated from the aircraft, which overturned after crashing landing. At least nine people were reported to be injured.

The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said in a statement that emergency crews are responding at the scene of the crash and that all flights to-and-from the Toronto airport have been temporarily halted. Delta said it is aware of the incident and “working to confirm details.” The crash comes following a winter storm that dumped more than eight inches of snow on the Toronto area.

Series of Crashes

The accident in Toronto is the latest in a series of recent airline crashes across North America. In January, a fatal midair collision occurred at Washington D.C.’s Reagan International Airport that killed 64 people. On Jan. 31, a regional jet that flew out of Northeast Philadelphia Airport crashed on the city’s streets shortly after takeoff.

Despite the recent problems, the FAA was just hit by a round of layoffs orchestrated by U.S. President Donald Trump and Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (D.O.G.E), with hundreds of qualified air traffic controllers having been fired suddenly and without notice.

DAL stock has risen 63% over the past 12 months.

Is DAL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Delta Air Lines has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 15 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy recommendations issued in the last three months. The average DAL price target of $83.07 implies 27.04% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on DAL stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: GM, F, STLA: Trump Says Auto Tariffs Are Coming April 2
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has said he plans to implement new tariffs on imported motor vehicles starting on April 2 of this year.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Trump’s comment on auto tariffs contained few other details, and did not say whether the new duties will apply to all automotive imports. Trump continues to advocate tariffs as a way to raise revenue, remedy trade imbalances, and pressure countries to act on U.S. concerns.

However, economists and industry analysts continue to sound the alarm, warning that tariffs raise prices for American consumers. Analysts and automotive executives have been particularly critical of potential tariffs on motor vehicles, saying they will sharply raise the price of cars sold in the U.S. and damage the auto manufacturing industry that is closely intertwined with Canada and Mexico.

Growing Concerns

About half of all the vehicles sold in the U.S. are manufactured within the country. The other 50% are imported from Mexico and Canada, as well as major auto-producing nations such as Japan, South Korea, and Germany.

The three major North American automotive manufacturers, General Motors (GM), Ford Motor Co. (F) and Stellantis (STLA) have raised concerns about potential tariffs, with Ford CEO Jim Farley being particularly vocal on the issue. Farley has criticized Trump’s proposed tariff of 25% on Mexico and Canada, saying it will “blow a hole” in the North American auto industry.

The American Automotive Policy Council, which represents GM, Ford and Stellantis, has publicly called for Trump to drop his proposed tariffs on Mexico and Canada, saying it could be potentially devastating.

Is F Stock a Buy?

The stock of Ford Motor Co. has a consensus Hold rating among 15 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy, eight Hold, and three Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average F price target of $10.56 implies 14.66% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on F stock
data.author: Joel Baglole
